title: Fix IC0508 canister-stopped error with frontend error handling and health check

requirements.text: In the admin Payments page (frontend/src/pages/admin/Payments.tsx) and Students page (frontend/src/pages/admin/Students.tsx), wrap all backend actor calls (approveUpiPayment, rejectUpiPayment, and any related payment approval methods) in try/catch blocks that specifically detect IC0508 / 'Canister is stopped' rejection errors. When this error is caught, display a clear, user-friendly toast or inline error message such as: 'The backend service is temporarily unavailable. Please try again shortly or contact support.'

quotes: Add better error handling on the frontend for IC0508 errors

acceptanceCriteria: When approveUpiPayment or rejectUpiPayment returns an IC0508 rejection, no unhandled error is thrown in the UI
A user-friendly error message is displayed informing the admin the canister is temporarily unavailable
The error message distinguishes IC0508 from other generic errors

requirements.text: Add a canister health check indicator to the AdminLayout (frontend/src/layouts/AdminLayout.tsx). On mount and periodically (every 60 seconds), ping the backend actor with a lightweight read call (e.g., a simple query to fetch a count or status). Display a small status badge in the admin sidebar/header: green 'Online' when the canister is reachable, and red 'Service Unavailable' when an IC0508 or network error is returned. This gives admins immediate visibility into backend availability before attempting approval actions.

quotes: Add a canister health check / status indicator so admins can see if the backend is reachable before attempting actions

acceptanceCriteria: Admin layout shows a health status badge (Online / Service Unavailable)
Status is checked on mount and every 60 seconds
IC0508 errors set the status to 'Service Unavailable' with a red indicator
Successful responses set the status to 'Online' with a green indicator

requirements.text: Review the approveUpiPayment method in backend/main.mo and ensure it does not contain any logic that could cause the canister to stop or trap unexpectedly (e.g., unguarded assertions, out-of-memory conditions, or explicit stop calls). Add defensive checks: validate that input parameters are non-empty before processing, and wrap any mutable state updates in safe patterns to prevent partial state corruption. The method should return a structured Result type (e.g., Result<Text, Text>) so callers can distinguish success from error without relying on traps.

quotes: Review the approveUpiPayment method for any logic that might trigger an unintended stop

acceptanceCriteria: approveUpiPayment returns a Result<Text, Text> or equivalent structured response instead of trapping
Empty or invalid input parameters are handled gracefully with an error result rather than an assertion trap
No explicit canister stop calls exist within the payment approval flow
The method compiles and deploys without errors

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Do not alter existing localStorage-based data flows unrelated to the payment approval fix
nonGoals: Topping up or managing canister cycles (infrastructure concern)
Rewriting the entire payment flow or data model
Adding new payment methods or changing UPI logic
